data.title: Simulations Plus Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31 beats by $0.05, revenue of $22.43M beats by $0.51M
data.description:
• Simulations Plus press release (NASDAQ:SLP): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $22.43M (+22.6% Y/Y) beats by $0.51M.
  • Software revenue increased 16% to $13.5 million, representing 60% of total revenue
  • Services revenue increased 34% to $8.9 million, representing 40% of total revenue
  • Gross profit was $13.1 million; gross margin was 59%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $6.6 million, representing 29% of total revenue, compared to $7.1 million, representing 39% of total revenue.
  • Fiscal 2025 Guidance
   

Fiscal 2025 Guidance

Revenue

  

$90M - $93M

Revenue growth

  

28 - 33%

Software mix

  

55 - 60%

Adjusted EBITDA margin

  

31 - 33%

Adjusted diluted EPS

  

$1.07 - $1.20
  • Shares +4.26%

data.title: Intel, TSMC consider joint venture to operate chipmaking facilities: report
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) have reached a preliminary agreement to form a joint venture to operate some of Intel's foundry facilities, according to a report by The Information.

TSMC would take a 20% stake in the new company, according to the report, which cited two people familiar with the situation. Intel, along with other unnamed U.S. semiconductor companies, would hold the majority of shares in the JV. In return for its stake, TSMC would provide training and insight into chipmaking techniques.

However, no final agreement has been reached, and some Intel executives have pushed back on the proposed JV, The Information reported.

Last month, investment firm Baird said it had heard discussions from the supply chain in Asia about a potential spin-off of Intel's foundry unit, with possible involvement by TSMC. 

"While there is no confirmation and potential completion of this project could be lengthy, we think this move makes sense, further building on Intel's prior CEO's focus on the company's core competency, manufacturing," Baird analyst Tristan Gerra wrote in a note to clients.

This followed a report from earlier in March that said TSMC had proposed a joint venture, in which it would operate Intel's factory, to several major U.S. chip manufacturers, including Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO).

TSMC plans to invest $100B in new capital in the U.S. over the next four years as the company looks to boost its presence there.

TSMC declined to comment on the report. Seeking Alpha also reached out to Intel for comment.

data.title: Coffee prices may see a jolt from tariff repercussions
data.description:
Coffee prices are expected to stay elevated as the market prepares for tariff-related volatility. New York futures for arabica are already near record highs due to weather issues and growing conditions in key regions, while robusta futures in London have also spiked over the last year.

On the tariff front, TD Cowen highlighted that the most notable U.S. coffee imports are derived from Brazil (10% tariff), Colombia (10% tariff), and Switzerland (31% tariff) that collectively made up 52% of 2024 U.S. coffee imports. There is also the shock 46% tariff on Vietnam exports to consider.

Based on the new tariffs announcements, analyst Andrew Charles and his team estimate a weighted average global coffee tariff of 14.5% that may or may not be passed on to consumers.

While coffee-related companies typically hedge many of their coffee costs, they could see an impact due to increased volatility and uncertainty in the market. In the restaurant sector, TD Cowen sees a negative adjusted EBITDA impact for Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX), Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS), and First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) if tariffs are unmitigated.

Other companies that could face coffee pricing headwinds include Coffee Holding (NASDAQ:JVA), Dunkin' Brands (DNKN), J.M Smucker (SJM), Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX), McDonald's (MCD), Costa Coffee (KO), Tim Hortons (QSR), Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), Kraft Heinz (KHC), Krispy Kreme (DNUT).

data.title: The USD:EUR tracks the Mag 7, a fall in one means a fall in the other – BCA Research
data.description:
The U.S. dollar/Euro (USD:EUR) is tracking the rise and fall of the Magnificent Seven, according to BCA Research.

 

 

 

Therefore, tactically, a relief rally in the Magnificent Seven stocks – (AAPL), (AMZN), (GOOG), (META), (MSFT), (NVDA), (TSLA) – will cause a relief rally in the U.S. dollar, which are down about 19% and 6% year-to-date, respectively, said Dhaval Joshi, chief strategist at BCA Research.

A relief rally in the dollar would also imply a tactical reversal in gold (GLD) – up 18% year-to-date – confirmed by gold’s 65-day price proximity, “reaching the point of collapse that has signaled previous tactical reversals,” he said.

data.title: France's aerospace industry is said to demand retaliatory tariffs on U.S.
data.description:
France’s aerospace sector has urged the European Commission to adopt “measured and decisive” counteractions if new U.S. tariffs inflict serious harm on one of the country's key export industries, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a source familiar with the issue.

The request came from Gifas, the influential French aerospace association currently chaired by Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY). The group emphasized that any retaliatory steps should be carefully designed to minimize unintended consequences for European firms that depend on imports from the United States.

While Gifas declined to comment to Reuters on the matter, the European Commission pointed to recent remarks by its president, Ursula von der Leyen, who stated the EU is ready to act if negotiations with the U.S. fail to yield results.

Earlier this week, President Donald Trump introduced broad tariffs on American imports, including a 20% levy on European Union products and a 10% tariff on goods from the United Kingdom, where Rolls-Royce (OTCPK:RYCEF) (OTCPK:RYCEY) is based.

The tone of the Gifas letter reflects France’s complex position within the global aerospace ecosystem. The country is a central hub in a vast international supply network for aircraft and engines.

Toulouse, France, is home to Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) — the world’s leading aircraft manufacturer — which competes globally with American rival Boeing (BA). Airbus operates across France, Germany, Spain and the UK. Additionally, France plays a critical role in CFM International, the world’s largest engine supplier by volume, which is a joint venture between Safran (OTCPK:SAFRF) (OTCPK:SAFRY) and GE Aerospace (GE). CFM provides engines to both Airbus and Boeing and has largely avoided trade conflicts in its decades-long history.

Airbus also sources engines from U.S.-based Pratt & Whitney, owned by RTX (RTX).

The aerospace sector ranks as France’s second-largest export industry, trailing only agri-food products.

Industry analysts suggest the tariffs could raise costs for American airlines, particularly on Airbus jets imported from Europe and the parts needed for ongoing aircraft maintenance.

Although earlier trade tensions focused on metals and automobiles, executives in the aerospace industry had previously downplayed the likelihood of aircraft becoming a direct target.

data.title: Rxsight adjusts 2025 revenue guidance to $160M-$175M amid premium IOL market challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: RxSight, Inc. (RXST) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Dr. Ron Kurtz highlighted that Q1 2025 revenue was impacted by a weakened premium intraocular lens (IOL) market, disruptions from sequential product launches by competitors, and macroeconomic effects on consumer sentiment. These factors led to a year-over-year decline in same-store sales of Light Adjustable Lenses (LALs).
  • Dr. Kurtz emphasized efforts to counteract these challenges, including refined clinical education and practice adoption programs for customers. Additionally, the company is leveraging clinical data to highlight the benefits of RxSight’s adjustable IOL platform.
  • CFO Shelley Thunen disclosed preliminary Q1 2025 revenue of $37.9 million, representing a 28% year-over-year increase but a sequential decline of 6% from Q4 2024. The installed base of Light Delivery Devices (LDDs) grew 43% year-over-year to 1,044 units.
  • CFO Thunen also announced revised 2025 revenue guidance to a range of $160 million to $175 million, down from the prior range of $185 million to $197 million. Operating expenses guidance was adjusted lower to $150 million to $160 million.

Outlook

  • The company revised full-year 2025 revenue guidance to $160 million to $175 million, reflecting a growth range of 14% to 25% compared to 2024.
  • Gross margin guidance remains unchanged at 71% to 73%, while operating expenses are now projected to be lower than previously estimated.
  • The company expressed uncertainty regarding the typical seasonal trends due to macroeconomic conditions and consumer sentiment, especially concerning the adoption of premium IOLs.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 preliminary revenue was $37.9 million, a year-over-year increase of 28%, but a 6% sequential decline from Q4 2024.
  • The company sold 73 LDDs in Q1 2025, bringing the total installed base to 1,044 units, marking a 43% increase year-over-year.
  • Sales of LALs reached 27,579 units in Q1 2025, up 36% year-over-year but down 5% sequentially.
  • The revised gross margin guidance remains consistent, with expectations of 71% to 73% for the full year 2025.

Q&A

  • Ryan Zimmerman, BTIG: Asked about productivity assumptions for Light Delivery Devices (LDDs) and the impact on guidance. CFO Shelley Thunen explained that guidance reflects changes in consumer sentiment and economic conditions, which may affect the number of Light Adjustable Lenses (LALs) sold per LDD.
  • Xuyang Li, Jefferies: Inquired about utilization trends among customer cohorts and potential pain points. CFO Thunen highlighted that utilization rates have been consistent across cohort years, but newer customers are ramping up more slowly in the current economic environment.
  • Steve Lichtman, Oppenheimer: Asked about efforts to accelerate new customer business models. CEO Kurtz discussed supporting third-party centralized treatment models as a way to expand access and adoption of RxSight’s technology.
  • Danielle Antalffy, UBS: Expressed concerns about the market saturation of the premium IOL segment. CEO Kurtz reaffirmed confidence in the untapped potential of the market and emphasized that macroeconomic and market disruptions are transitory.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed cautious sentiment, focusing on the revised guidance and concerns over market saturation and macroeconomic impacts on adoption rates. Skepticism was evident in questions related to productivity and new customer adoption.
  • Management maintained a slightly defensive tone but expressed confidence in long-term growth strategies, emphasizing innovation and market expansion efforts. Dr. Kurtz stated, “We believe that these factors are likely transitory and that we remain well-positioned for future growth.”

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased sequentially from $40.2 million in Q4 2024 to $37.9 million in Q1 2025.
  • Guidance for 2025 was lowered significantly from the prior range of $185 million to $197 million to $160 million to $175 million, reflecting a more conservative growth outlook.
  • The tone of management shifted from confidence in previous quarters to acknowledging challenges and focusing on mitigation strategies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges in the premium IOL market, including competitive product launches and macroeconomic impacts on consumer sentiment, were highlighted as significant risks.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the potential market saturation and slower adoption rates among new customers.
  • Management outlined mitigation strategies, including enhanced customer education programs, leveraging clinical data, and exploring new customer business models.

Final Takeaway

RxSight reported a challenging Q1 2025, citing macroeconomic pressures and competitive market dynamics as key factors impacting performance. Management adjusted the full-year revenue guidance to $160 million to $175 million and emphasized efforts to drive growth through refined education programs, product enhancements, and support for innovative business models. While analysts expressed caution, RxSight remains focused on leveraging its proprietary adjustable IOL technology to expand market penetration and sustain long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Senate panel backs Paul Atkins as Trump's SEC pick
data.description:
The U.S. Senate Banking Committee voted Thursday to advance President Donald Trump's nominees to lead the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency.

In a hearing, the panel voted 13-11 in a party-line vote to approve Paul Atkins as SEC chair and Jonathan Gould as OCC chief.

Now, the nominations await full Senate approval, which would allow Atkins, a former SEC commissioner, and Gould, a former chief counsel at the OCC, to start leading their respective agencies.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Plus free for college students in US, Canada
data.description:
OpenAI has made ChatGPT Plus free for college students residing in the U.S. and Canada for a limited time.

"ChatGPT Plus is free for college students in the US and Canada through May!," CEO Sam Altman posted on X today.  

A subscription to ChatGPT Plus costs $20 per month, while ChatGPT Pro goes for $200 per month. The offering might allow the Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) startup to reel in more paying subscribers once the free period ends.  

ChatGPT, the generative artificial intelligence chatbot, has added new paid subscribers at a quick clip, as it continues to add new tools and add-ons to its flagship product. Its latest image generation tool, released last week, prompted a swarm of new users eager to download the app. Global app downloads and weekly active users on the app grew 11% and 5%, respectively, from the prior week, while in-app purchase revenue increased 6%. ChatGPT also surpassed 150M weekly active users for the first time this year.

ChatGPT also recently surpassed 20M paid subscribers. That's a three-month jump of 4.5M paid subscribers, or roughly 30%. This suggests the company is generating at least $415M in revenue per month, up from at least $333M per month at the end of last year. OpenAI expects its revenue to more than triple in 2025 and reach $12.7B.

OpenAI's recent $40B funding round, which was done in partnership with SoftBank Group (OTCPK:SFTBY), has given the company a $300B valuation. However, Softbank introduced a caveat requiring OpenAI to transition to a for-profit company by the end of 2025 to secure all the new funding, according to reports.

data.title: Maersk says Trump tariffs will hurt global trade, delay goods in transit
data.description:
Shipping company A.P. Moeller-Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) warned Thursday that the sweeping tariffs announced by the Trump administration will hurt the global economy and delay goods currently in transit, Dow Jones reported.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) said it expects customers - which include top retailers such as Amazon, Walmart and Target - will be more cautious about inventory levels and delay clearing goods through customs as they wait for clarity on the tariffs.

Trump's move to slap a minimum 10% tariff on most goods imported into the U.S. as well as much higher levies on dozens of other countries "clearly isn't good news for [the] global economy, stability and trade," Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) said, but it is "still too early to say with any confidence how this ultimately will unfold."

"In the very short term, it is likely we will see an increase in demand for bonded storage as customers will want to hold off clearing goods while they get more certainty," the company said.

Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) shares slid more than 9% in Copenhagen on Thursday, and European shipping peer Hapag-Lloyd (OTCPK:HLAGF) (OTCPK:HPGLY) fell 9% in Frankfurt.

Separately, Canadian Pacific Kansas City (CP) said on Wednesday it and Lanco Group said it agreed to sell the 47-mile Panama Canal Railway Company to a Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) unit; financial terms were not disclosed.

President Trump has complained for months about Chinese influence of the Panama Canal, and the deal somewhat undermines the U.S. presence along the key trade route.

data.title: Twilio in focus on report of data hack impacting nearly 850K customers
data.description:
• Twilio (NYSE:TWLO) was in focus on Thursday after HackRead reported that the communications software suffered a data breach that saw the data records of 848,000 of its customers sold.
  • The breach occurred at Twilio's SendGrid, which is a cloud-based email delivery platform, the news outlet added.
  • Data that may have been impacted include customer emails, phone numbers, physical addresses, as well as certain company-level data, such as revenue, employee counts and rankings. Other pertinent financial information, such as operating income, and net income may have also been impacted.
  • Twilio shares fell more than 9% on Thursday as part of a broader market sell off in response to President Trump's tariffs.
